tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Cricketer Reunites With the Stranger Who Saved His Life
stories.summary: Muhammad Akhtar Hussain embraced Laura Anderson, who performed 29 minutes of CPR to save his life after he collapsed on the cricket pitch, calling her his guardian angel.

stories.headline: Four Children Given a Second Chance: New Cancer Cell Therapy Delivers Hope
stories.summary: Four children with terminal brain cancers are thriving years after experimental immune therapy, with three showing no evidence of disease—truly awesome, says Gene Hwang.

stories.headline: First Woman Test Centurion at Lord's: 'Best Is Yet to Come'
stories.summary: Yastika Bhatia scored a historic 113-run Test century at Lord's, becoming the first woman to achieve this milestone just six months after a career-threatening knee injury.

stories.headline: Man Travels 1,200 km to Repay a 30-Year-Old Loan With Interest
stories.summary: After three decades, a man used Google to find his old friend and repay a forgotten loan with interest, proving integrity has no expiration date.

stories.headline: 'It's an Army': Community Rushes to Aid Flood Victims
stories.summary: After 13 inches of rain flooded Reynolds County, Missouri, Dana Meyers coordinated volunteers at Lesterville High School who spread out water-damaged photographs and delivered medicine to isolated families.

stories.headline: Volleyball Team Wins National Championship in Honor of Fallen Father
stories.summary: Peak Volleyball's 13U team dedicated their national championship to Marty Proper, their biggest fan who suffered a heart attack on the way home from a qualifier.

stories.headline: Fisherman Gently Releases Baby Dugong: 'Run, Run, Golden Child'
stories.summary: A tender moment as a fisherman releases a rescued baby dugong back to the sea, embodying years of community marine conservation care.

stories.headline: Born Without Hands, He Became a Football Freestyle Star
stories.summary: Blessing Saju mastered football freestyle and martial arts despite being born handless, inspiring millions as 'Cristiano Saju' on social media.

stories.headline: A Stranger's Social Media Post Sparked a Guitar Miracle
stories.summary: Stefan Roberts posted about Lucas's stolen guitar on social media, and within hours the community rallied to replace it, bringing a huge smile to his face.

stories.headline: Thailand's Women Make History at Special Olympics Unified Football World Cup
stories.summary: Thailand's women's unified football team won bronze at the Special Olympics World Cup in Paris and received the Most Unified Team Award, featuring seven athletes with and without intellectual disabilities.

stories.headline: Humanoid Robot Performs Surgery, Opening Doors for Remote Care
stories.summary: Surgeons successfully controlled a humanoid robot to perform minimally invasive surgery, a breakthrough for bringing expert care to underserved communities.

stories.headline: Endangered Loggerheads Given Hope: Tracked Release in Türkiye
stories.summary: Two loggerhead sea turtles named Flora and Fauna are released with satellite trackers, giving researchers new tools to protect this vulnerable species.

stories.headline: New Device Pulls Carbon From the Air Using Salt and Electricity
stories.summary: Engineers Paul Rozzi, Kyle Smith, and Jeong Lee developed a battery-type device that captures CO₂ from the air using saltwater chemistry and electricity.
